# Proverbs 26

## Verses (28)

| # | Basic English Bible | Compare |
|---|---|---|
| [1](BBE.md#v1) | Like snow in summer and rain when the grain is being cut, so honour is not natural for the foolish. | [WEB](WEB.md#v1) · [KJV](KJV.md#v1) · [ASV](ASV.md#v1) |
| [2](BBE.md#v2) | As the sparrow in her wandering and the swallow in her flight, so the curse does not come without a cause. | [WEB](WEB.md#v2) · [KJV](KJV.md#v2) · [ASV](ASV.md#v2) |
| [3](BBE.md#v3) | A whip for the horse, a mouth-bit for the ass, and a rod for the back of the foolish. | [WEB](WEB.md#v3) · [KJV](KJV.md#v3) · [ASV](ASV.md#v3) |
| [4](BBE.md#v4) | Do not give to the foolish man a foolish answer, or you will be like him. | [WEB](WEB.md#v4) · [KJV](KJV.md#v4) · [ASV](ASV.md#v4) |
| [5](BBE.md#v5) | Give a foolish man a foolish answer, or he will seem wise to himself. | [WEB](WEB.md#v5) · [KJV](KJV.md#v5) · [ASV](ASV.md#v5) |
| [6](BBE.md#v6) | He who sends news by the hand of a foolish man is cutting off his feet and drinking in damage. | [WEB](WEB.md#v6) · [KJV](KJV.md#v6) · [ASV](ASV.md#v6) |
| [7](BBE.md#v7) | The legs of one who has no power of walking are hanging loose; so is a wise saying in the mouth of the foolish. | [WEB](WEB.md#v7) · [KJV](KJV.md#v7) · [ASV](ASV.md#v7) |
| [8](BBE.md#v8) | Giving honour to a foolish man is like attempting to keep a stone fixed in a cord. | [WEB](WEB.md#v8) · [KJV](KJV.md#v8) · [ASV](ASV.md#v8) |
| [9](BBE.md#v9) | Like a thorn which goes up into the hand of a man overcome by drink, so is a wise saying in the mouth of a foolish man. | [WEB](WEB.md#v9) · [KJV](KJV.md#v9) · [ASV](ASV.md#v9) |
| [10](BBE.md#v10) | Like an archer wounding all who go by, is a foolish man overcome by drink. | [WEB](WEB.md#v10) · [KJV](KJV.md#v10) · [ASV](ASV.md#v10) |
| [11](BBE.md#v11) | Like a dog going back to the food which he has not been able to keep down, is the foolish man doing his foolish acts over again. | [WEB](WEB.md#v11) · [KJV](KJV.md#v11) · [ASV](ASV.md#v11) |
| [12](BBE.md#v12) | Have you seen a man who seems to himself to be wise? There is more hope for the foolish than for him. | [WEB](WEB.md#v12) · [KJV](KJV.md#v12) · [ASV](ASV.md#v12) |
| [13](BBE.md#v13) | The hater of work says, There is a lion in the way; a lion is in the streets. | [WEB](WEB.md#v13) · [KJV](KJV.md#v13) · [ASV](ASV.md#v13) |
| [14](BBE.md#v14) | A door is turned on its pillar, and the hater of work on his bed. | [WEB](WEB.md#v14) · [KJV](KJV.md#v14) · [ASV](ASV.md#v14) |
| [15](BBE.md#v15) | The hater of work puts his hand deep into the basin: lifting it again to his mouth is a weariness to him. | [WEB](WEB.md#v15) · [KJV](KJV.md#v15) · [ASV](ASV.md#v15) |
| [16](BBE.md#v16) | The hater of work seems to himself wiser than seven men who are able to give an answer with good sense. | [WEB](WEB.md#v16) · [KJV](KJV.md#v16) · [ASV](ASV.md#v16) |
| [17](BBE.md#v17) | He who gets mixed up in a fight which is not his business, is like one who takes a dog by the ears while it is going by. | [WEB](WEB.md#v17) · [KJV](KJV.md#v17) · [ASV](ASV.md#v17) |
| [18](BBE.md#v18) | As one who is off his head sends about flaming sticks and arrows of death, | [WEB](WEB.md#v18) · [KJV](KJV.md#v18) · [ASV](ASV.md#v18) |
| [19](BBE.md#v19) | So is the man who gets the better of his neighbour by deceit, and says, Am I not doing so in sport? | [WEB](WEB.md#v19) · [KJV](KJV.md#v19) · [ASV](ASV.md#v19) |
| [20](BBE.md#v20) | Without wood, the fire goes out; and where there is no secret talk, argument is ended. | [WEB](WEB.md#v20) · [KJV](KJV.md#v20) · [ASV](ASV.md#v20) |
| [21](BBE.md#v21) | Like breath on coals and wood on fire, so a man given to argument gets a fight started. | [WEB](WEB.md#v21) · [KJV](KJV.md#v21) · [ASV](ASV.md#v21) |
| [22](BBE.md#v22) | The words of one who says evil of his neighbour secretly are like sweet food, they go down into the inner parts of the stomach. | [WEB](WEB.md#v22) · [KJV](KJV.md#v22) · [ASV](ASV.md#v22) |
| [23](BBE.md#v23) | Smooth lips and an evil heart are like a vessel of earth plated with silver waste. | [WEB](WEB.md#v23) · [KJV](KJV.md#v23) · [ASV](ASV.md#v23) |
| [24](BBE.md#v24) | With his lips the hater makes things seem what they are not, but deceit is stored up inside him; | [WEB](WEB.md#v24) · [KJV](KJV.md#v24) · [ASV](ASV.md#v24) |
| [25](BBE.md#v25) | When he says fair words, have no belief in him; for in his heart are seven evils: | [WEB](WEB.md#v25) · [KJV](KJV.md#v25) · [ASV](ASV.md#v25) |
| [26](BBE.md#v26) | Though his hate is covered with deceit, his sin will be seen openly before the meeting of the people. | [WEB](WEB.md#v26) · [KJV](KJV.md#v26) · [ASV](ASV.md#v26) |
| [27](BBE.md#v27) | He who makes a hole in the earth will himself go falling into it: and on him by whom a stone is rolled the stone will come back again. | [WEB](WEB.md#v27) · [KJV](KJV.md#v27) · [ASV](ASV.md#v27) |
| [28](BBE.md#v28) | A false tongue has hate for those who have clean hearts, and a smooth mouth is a cause of falling. | [WEB](WEB.md#v28) · [KJV](KJV.md#v28) · [ASV](ASV.md#v28) |
